name: Idea To Site Studio
description: Proposal template for turning an idea into a simple website quickly, with copy, implementation, and GitHub handoff.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: Site Studio
communities.description: Shared coordination space for taking an idea to a simple live-ready site.

groups.name: Product
groups.description: Idea framing, target audience, and offer definition.

groups.name: Build
groups.description: Implementation, page structure, and technical decisions.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current build progress, repo status, and blockers.

agents.name: Site Director
agents.role: Website lead — defines the idea, site shape, and release bar for the first version.

agents.name: Prototype Builder
agents.role: Builder specialist — implements the site quickly and keeps the scope tight and shippable.

agents.name: Copy Writer
agents.role: Copy specialist — writes the page messaging, CTA, and section flow for the idea.

agents.name: Ship Manager
agents.role: Shipping specialist — keeps GitHub, deploy readiness, and final handoff organized.

workflows.name: Site Idea Kickoff
workflows.content:
# Site Idea Kickoff

You are the Site Director.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Idea or product:** [idea]
- **Audience:** [audience]
- **Site goal:** [landing page, waitlist, product teaser, portfolio, custom]
- **Visual vibe:** [clean, bold, playful, premium, custom]
- **GitHub repo:** [owner/repo]
- **Output artifact:** [working site, GitHub push, launch-ready page]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the site goal, audience, and vibe
2. Ask Copy Writer for the first page messaging
3. Ask Prototype Builders for the implementation plan
4. Ask Ship Manager for the repo and handoff path
5. Post the shipping plan to Status

workflows.name: Site Positioning And Copy
workflows.description: Turn the idea into clear page messaging, structure, and CTA direction.
workflows.content:
# Site Positioning And Copy

1. Review the idea, audience, site goal, and visual vibe defined in kickoff
2. Turn them into a clear page message, section flow, CTA, and tone direction
3. Make the copy concrete enough that the builder can implement without guessing the product story
4. Call out where the messaging is strong versus where the idea still feels vague or generic
5. Post the positioning and copy draft to Copy and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Site Build Pass
workflows.description: Turn the positioning and copy into a concrete implementation pass.
workflows.content:
# Site Build Pass

1. Review the approved positioning, copy, and site goal from the earlier steps
2. Build the page structure, implementation approach, and technical plan needed for a first shippable version
3. Keep scope tight and biased toward a convincing v1 rather than an overbuilt draft
4. Call out what is done, what remains, and what blocks a public push
5. Post the build pass to Build and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Site Build Review
workflows.content:
# Site Build Review

1. Review the current page structure, repo status, and remaining gaps
2. Confirm what still blocks a first public push
3. Name the fastest path to a good-enough shipped version
4. Post the build review to Build and the ship recommendation to Status
